# GameTan — Esports Talent Detection

> GameTan measures real gaming talent through cognitive science mini-games and compares results to pro player benchmarks.

## What is GameTan?
GameTan (gametan.ai) is an esports talent assessment platform. Players complete scientifically-designed mini-games that measure cognitive abilities essential for competitive gaming: reaction speed, pattern recognition, spatial awareness, memory, decision speed, rhythm sense, impulse control, visual attention, multitasking, emotional control, risk assessment, and teamwork cognition.

## How it works
1. Choose a tier: Quick (3 games, 3 min, free), Standard (7 games, 10 min, free), or Pro (17 games, 25 min, $3.99)
2. Play cognitive mini-games based on published research (Stroop, Go/No-Go, UFOV, N-Back, Flanker, MOT, etc.)
3. Get your talent profile with percentile scores and pro player comparison
4. Discover your gamer archetype (16 types based on talent distribution)
5. AI Coach analyzes your results and recommends games that match your strengths

## Key pages
- Homepage: https://gametan.ai/
- Start quiz: https://gametan.ai/quiz
- Browse games: https://gametan.ai/explore
- Gamer archetypes: https://gametan.ai/archetype
- Blog: https://gametan.ai/blog
- Privacy: https://gametan.ai/privacy
- Terms: https://gametan.ai/terms

## Scientific basis
Games are based on established cognitive science paradigms:
- Reaction Speed: Simple/Choice RT (Bridges et al. 2020)
- Stroop Task: Cognitive interference (Stroop 1935; MacLeod 1991)
- Go/No-Go: Impulse control (Donders 1869; Logan 1994)
- UFOV: Visual attention breadth (Ball et al. 1988)
- N-Back: Working memory (Owen et al. 2005; Jaeggi et al. 2010)
- Flanker: Interference control (Eriksen & Eriksen 1974)
- MOT: Visual tracking (Pylyshyn & Storm 1988)
- Task Switch: Cognitive flexibility (Monsell 2003)
- BART: Risk assessment (Lejuez et al. 2002)
- Dual-Task: Attention allocation (Pashler 1994)
- Perspective Taking: Theory of mind (Michelon & Zacks 2006)
- Corsi Block: Short-term memory (Kessels et al. 2000)
- Mental Rotation: Spatial reasoning (Shepard & Metzler 1971)

## Pricing
- Free: Quick Test (3 games) and Standard Test (7 games)
- Pro Assessment: $3.99 one-time, 365 days — full 17-dimension analysis, AI coaching, PDF report
